question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findById() returns a Topic entity with a feeExemptKeyList that contains a key that has been rotated or deleted on-chain, causing the fee calculator to incorrectly exempt users who no longer hold the exempt key, permanently bypassing custom fee charges and freezing collector revenue?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findById() does not validate the size of the feeExemptKeyList bytes, allowing a topic with an extremely large feeExemptKeyList to cause memory exhaustion in the fee calculator when deserialized, permanently blocking fee assessment for that topic and freezing all custom fee flows?
